draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer friendly, approachable copy that feels like talking to a colleague rather than reading documentation
facade.axisTargeted: voice and personality — friendly vs professional
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warmth, humor, casual language, contractions, and human-centered microcopy throughout the interface
facade.rejectImplies: Maintain formal, instructional tone; prioritize clarity and precision over personality; use official terminology and passive voice

evidence.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer friendly, approachable copy that feels like talking to a colleague rather than reading documentation
evidence.implication: Lean into warmth, humor, casual language, contractions, and human-centered microcopy throughout the interface

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer low-light interfaces that reduce eye strain during extended focus sessions
facade.label: Dark mode workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Explore darker color palettes, high-contrast text, dim backgrounds as primary aesthetic direction
facade.rejectImplies: Explore bright, high-key color palettes and light backgrounds as more appropriate for the workspace aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer motion that feels like objects existing in space rather than appearing instantly
facade.axisTargeted: Transition style: gradual entrance vs instant pop
facade.label: Buttons fade in
facade.acceptImplies: Embrace entrance animations and layered reveal sequences across the workspace
facade.rejectImplies: Favor instant-state UI where elements are already present or use cut-based transitions instead

facade.hypothesis: The user may prefer organic, approachable aesthetics over sharp geometric precision
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should feel welcoming and less industrial—soften hard edges, use curves and natural forms
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor clean, precise, modernist geometry—sharp corners and grid-aligned layouts signal clarity and efficiency

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer dense information (tables, spreadsheets, structured data) over narrative explanations or metrics cards
facade.axisTargeted: Information density—compact reference vs. scannable summaries
facade.label: Data table or summary card
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace rewards fast lookup, comparison, and precision. Design should favor tables, lists, detailed views, minimal prose.
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace favors quick orientation and understanding. Design should favor cards, key metrics, narrative context, whitespace.

facade.hypothesis: Users working in an AI workspace benefit from persistent, always-visible navigation and tool panels rather than collapsible or tab-based alternatives
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence vs. screen real estate trade-off
facade.label: Sidebar that stays put
facade.acceptImplies: Explore fixed sidebars with various widths and content densities; test whether users want consistent left/right anchor points for tools, chat history, or prompts
facade.rejectImplies: Explore tab systems, hamburger menus, or modal panels; users may prefer maximized canvas space and on-demand navigation

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, distraction-free visual design vs. rich, detailed interface aesthetics for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible and organized in fixed panels vs. hidden/floating and revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, professional communication in their workspace
Whether user wants a calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, stimulating visual environment for productivity
Whether user prefers information presented in dense, comprehensive views vs. progressive disclosure with one thing at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step

axes.label: Whether user wants instant, snappy responses to actions vs. smooth, animated transitions that feel deliberate

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, distraction-free visual design vs. rich, detailed interface aesthetics for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible and organized in fixed panels vs. hidden/floating and revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, professional communication in their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, stimulating visual environment for productivity
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers information presented in dense, comprehensive views vs. progressive disclosure with one thing at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant, snappy responses to actions vs. smooth, animated transitions that feel deliberate
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace need confident, non-technical language that explains features without requiring AI literacy — avoiding terms like 'prompt engineering,' 'embedding,' or 'fine-tuning' unless absolutely necessary.
facade.axisTargeted: Accessibility vs. Technical depth — does copy assume expertise or welcome newcomers?
facade.label: Jargon-free explanations
facade.acceptImplies: Strip technical vocabulary from all UI copy, error messages, and onboarding; prioritize plain language even when describing complex features.
facade.rejectImplies: Assume users have baseline AI knowledge; use precise technical terminology; reserve plain language for marketing only, not product interface.

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
